item.title: Bacchus WJB-1MS-DX-AC RSM/M - 3TS(3tone burst) — $363.02
item.description:
$363.02 · Brand New · SOAR


[ Shop Policy & Product Photos ]


Professional Setup Included: As our store's original service, every guitar and bass undergoes a final inspection and setup adjustment immediately before shipping. This ensures you can start playing with minimal adjustment needed, saving you the hassle of a full setup from scratch.


About the Photos:


If the listing title contains a serial number or a management number: The photos show the actual instrument you will receive.




If there is no specific number in the title: The images are sample/stock photos. However, we are happy to take photos of the actual unit upon request. Please feel free to contact us!


---

~ WJB-1MS-DX-AC RSM/M (Bacchus) ~

● Multi-Scale Model

"Multi-scale" refers to a construction that combines multiple scale lengths. This model features a design where the scale length increases incrementally, from 34 inches on the 1st string to 35 inches on the 4th string. This provides tighter, more solid tension on the lower strings, allowing you to play with optimal tension even when using down-tunings like Drop D.

By keeping the higher strings at a standard 34-inch scale, this bass achieves an exceptionally balanced tension across the entire fretboard. Furthermore, the visual aesthetic retains the familiar look of a traditional bass, ensuring that players accustomed to standard basses can transition seamlessly without any discomfort. These features make it the perfect, worry-free choice as an introductory multi-scale instrument.

● Active Circuit

Equipped with an active circuit featuring a 2-band EQ, this model allows for precise control over both treble and bass frequencies. It accommodates detailed sound shaping, enabling you to dial in the perfect tone for any musical genre or playing style.


● Single-String Bridge

To accommodate the multi-scale design, this model is equipped with single-string individual bridges that allow the scale position of each string to be set independently. This independent bridge system achieves the perfect balance between string tension and overall playability. *Please select long-scale strings when changing strings on this model.


● Stainless Steel Frets

This model features stainless steel frets, known for their excellent wear resistance and bright, crisp note articulation. The jumbo-sized frets facilitate effortless fretting and deliver rich, extended sustain, making this bass highly versatile across a wide range of genres.


● Heel-less Cut

An uncommon feature for entry-level models, the heel-less cut joint allows for smooth fingering and comfortable access even when playing in the higher frets. Gentle contouring around the neck joint bush bolts further enhances overall playability.


● Roasted Maple Neck

Roasted Maple is a material produced by treating maple wood at high temperatures to volatilize its internal moisture and oils, artificially simulating the aging process. Aged wood is highly resistant to warping, twisting, and seasonal movement. When utilized for the neck, the benefits extend beyond mere stability to the sound itself, yielding a rich acoustic resonance and a mature, broken-in tone reminiscent of a vintage instrument. While typically reserved for high-end models, we have successfully implemented this premium material at a highly accessible price point. *Please note that the degree of color change from the roasting process and the grain of the maple will vary from instrument to instrument.


● Spec List

Body: Poplar N

eck: Roasted Maple

Fingerboard: Roasted Maple

Nut: PPS

Bridge: Single String Type

Machineheads: Open Gear Type

Fret: Stainless Steel

Pickup: Original JB Type

Control: 1Vol, 1Balancer, Treble, Bass

Preamp: Original Scale: 1st string: 864mm (34 inch) / 4th string: 889mm (35 inch)

Fingerboard Radius: 305R

Width at Nut: 38.0mm (*)

Finish: Polyurethane or Polyester

Strings: Roundwound .045 - .105

Accessories: Gig bag, Warranty card, Wrenches

Product design, appearance, and specifications are subject to change without notice. *The neck width at the nut is measured perpendicular to the strings.

*Please select long-scale strings when changing strings on this model.

International Buyers - Please Note:


Manufacturer warranty for products imported from Japan is valid only for customers residing in Japan. Import duties, taxes, and charges are not included in the item price or shipping cost. These charges are the buyer's responsibility. Please check with your country's customs office to determine what these additional costs will be prior to buying.

item.title: 【new】AV BASSES / AVJ5m Burl Poplar 4.025kg #AVJ5m-2025-01【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $5,950
item.description:
$5,950 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

AV Basses is a high-end Czech brand that gained significant attention when Jamiroquai bassist Paul Turner began using their basses.

The brand was started in the 1990s by professional bassist Ales Vychodil.

At the time, major instrument manufacturers supplied numerous basses to the market, but none could meet his requirements.

To satisfy his own needs, he began building custom basses himself.

In the 2000s, he started OEM manufacturing for boutique brands in Europe and America, showcasing his high level of manufacturing skill.

I met builder Ales at the Guitar Summit in Germany in 2019.

He is a wonderful person, with a strong spirit of challenge, yet always considerate of the musician.

I have placed several orders with him since 2019, and he has consistently provided high-quality instruments.

This consistency demonstrates the brand's strength and is universally important.

This model was exhibited at the 2025 Guitar Summit.

Basically, it's based on the JB style, but actively incorporates modern specifications.

The body top is made of Burl Poplar, and the back is made of Swamp Ash.

Both have very beautiful wood grain.

The neck is made of Roasted Birdseye Maple, and the fingerboard is Macassar Ebony.

A very luxurious wood combination.

The tuning machines and bridge are made by HIPSHOT.

The pickups are Nordstrand FatStack, and the preamp is also a Nordstrand 3B.

The controls are master volume (push/pull - active/passive), balancer, middle, and treble/bass.

Sound-wise, it feels smooth and wide-ranging.

Yet it also possesses a thick and powerful nuance.

It gives the impression of a properly modernized update of the JB, and I feel it's a comfortable instrument for many modern professional bassists.

SPECS

Scale : 34"" (864 mm)
Wood for the body: Burl Poplar top, Rosewood layer, Swamp Ash back.
Wood for the neck: 1 piece roasted Birdseye Maple.
Fingerboard : Macassar Ebony. Pearl side and top dots. Ebony nut. 21 medium jumbo frets 2.4 mm.
Pickups : Nordstrand FatStack set of pickups.
Hardware : Hipshot A Style bridge 18 mm (.708"") string spacing, Hipshot Ultralite tuners 3/8"" posts Lollipop, black.
Electronic: Active/passive Nordstrand 3b, 3 band
Controls : 1x main volume with push pull to passive, 1x blend, 1x EQ Mid (tone control in passive), 1x stacked EQ Treble/Bass, output jack 6.3 mm Stereo.
Strings: DR Pure Blues Nickel Roundwounds 045-125 gauge
Finish: High glossy lacquer on the body and headstock, satin matte neck.

As this is a show model, there may be some minor scratches and dents.
Please understand.

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: None

Text: Ryoma Terao

Inspector: Ishikawa

Product Photos: Lee

Manufacturer's warranty included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases within Japan)

Shipped in a sturdy cardboard box.

International shipping is available.

item will be packed with a hard cardboard box.
we do ship internationally.

Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

item.title: Heartfield DR5 1990 - Flame maple Red — $1,100
item.description:
$1,100 · Good · Low Rider

For Sale: Rare 1990 Heartfield DR-5C (Made in Japan)

Up for sale is a rare Heartfield DR-5C from 1990. Heartfield was a high-end sub-brand designed in collaboration with Fender, manufactured at the legendary Hirooka factory (Fujigen) between 1988 and 1991. Sporting a modern body shape, this series shares a very similar vibe and aesthetic to the early-era Yamaha TRB.

While this series came in both bolt-on and neck-through configurations, this particular model is the premium Custom version featuring a 5-piece neck-through-body construction.

Key Specs:

Body: Ash with a Flame Maple top

Neck/Fingerboard: 5-piece neck-through with a Rosewood fretboard (24 frets)

Hardware: Gold Gotoh hardware (except bridge, see condition notes)

Pickups: Low-impedance EMG-designed single-coils (hum-canceling)

Electronics: Active/Passive bypass switch, XLR DI output jack.

Preamp: Unique preamp design heavily inspired by Alembic. It features a sweepable filter control that shapes your tone to fit any genre with a single knob—all without losing the bass's natural character. Switching to passive (bypass) delivers a classic, sweet Jazz Bass tone.

Condition & Playability:

Overall Condition: Excellent given its age. There are some touch-up spots (as shown in the photos) and minor oxidation/patina on the gold hardware.

Bridge: The bridge has been upgraded to a black one of the exact same dimensions as the original.

Playability: The neck is in superb shape with a fully functional truss rod. It is currently set up with very low action, making it incredibly easy to play with zero fret buzz.

Electronics: 100% functional. The tone is exceptionally clean with virtually zero noise/hum, making it an absolute beast in the studio or live on stage.

Sound & Verdict:

This bass is incredibly versatile. Its slap tone easily rivals a vintage Jazz Bass but comes with a modern edge and a highly defined, tight Low B string.

If you have been hunting for a Yamaha TRB-5P (or TRB P2) but don’t want to pay those sky-high prices, this Heartfield DR-5C is the ultimate alternative. It offers the same premium neck-through construction and immense tonal versatility at half the price.

Comes with a high-quality padded gig bag.

item.title: Greco LGB-100 Les Paul Rock Spirits – MIJ – 2000 — $1,514.33
item.description:
$1,514.33 · Very Good · Gruuvik Chamber

Description

Japanese-made Greco LGB-100 Les Paul Bass from the Rock Spirits series, representing one of the higher-tier MIJ interpretations of the Les Paul bass concept around 2000.

Specifications

Model: LGB-100 “Rock Spirit”
Serial: A006709 (January 2000 production; A = January, “00” = year)
Construction: Set neck
Body: Carved maple top / Mahogany back
Neck: Mahogany
Fingerboard: Rosewood
Scale: 32″ (medium scale)
Frets: 24
Pickups: 2 × Greco GH-B1 humbuckers
Controls: 2 volume, 2 tone, 3-way selector (Les Paul layout)
Weight: 4.9 kg
Strings: New La Bella 760FS-S Standard tension Flatwounds 45-105

Condition

Very good overall condition for a 2000 production instrument.

Cosmetic wear consistent with use. Structurally solid with no evidence of repairs. Electronics function correctly.

There are small cracks in the neck pickup mounting ring. The ring remains stable and fully functional, and the pickup is securely mounted.

Notes on the Model

Models like the LGB-100 are relatively uncommon outside Japan and are rarely encountered on the European market.

A well-built and tonally distinctive MIJ Les Paul bass that stands apart from more common short-scale alternatives.

At 4.9 kg, this is actually on the lighter side for Les Paul-style basses, which are typically known for their substantial weight due to dense mahogany construction and carved tops — while still delivering the expected low-end authority and sustain.

Tonally, this bass delivers a thick, powerful humbucker sound with pronounced low-mid presence — classic Les Paul-style character with slightly tighter response thanks to the 32″ scale.

Shipping

Ships from the European Union (Estonia), with no import duties for EU buyers.

The instrument will be carefully packed to ensure safe transit and shipped via tracked courier service. Fully insured or express shipping options (DHL, UPS, FedEx) are available upon request.

Minor setup adjustments may be required after climate adaptation.

Please feel free to message with any questions or if additional photographs are required.

More soul-approved vintage Japanese and handpicked basses available — no hype, just the good ones.

item.title: Kikuchi Guitars Hermes RV5 PASSIVE OWH / Olympic White【3.88kg】 — $2,588.84
item.description:
$2,588.84 · Brand New · KUROSAWA MUSIC G-CLUB UMEDA

Welcome to Kurosawa Music G-CLUB UMEDA!!

We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Please contact us for more information.


*Important Notice!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we cannot accept PayPal Guest Checkout.

Please create a PayPal account before making payment.

Thank you for viewing! Feel free to contact us for details.

Also, please be sure to read our store policies listed below the instrument description!

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――――


Mr. Kikuchi, who studied under Roger Sadowsky in New York and went on to popularize the Japanese-made Sadowsky brand, has launched a new brand: “Kikuchi Guitars.”

The “Hermes Series” features a solid body with a moderate weight and a compact “dinky” shape designed with modern tone and playability in mind, pursuing the “ease of play” and “sound” that players seek.

The neck grip, featuring a narrower nut width than a standard J-style, makes it easier to grip, enabling smooth playability from the low to the high frets. Furthermore, Mr. Kikuchi’s attention to detail is evident in the design, which enhances neck strength and incorporates a headstock with just the right thickness to ensure durability.


While Kikuchi is often associated with modern active basses in the Sadowsky tradition, this model is passive. The pickups, newly developed specifically for this passive model, feature the classic configuration of two volume controls and one tone control. They allow you to directly experience the natural resonance of the wood—a hallmark of passive systems—and offer such superb balance that you won’t even feel the need for an active circuit.


We invite you to experience its proven quality and sound for yourself.


Body: Alder

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Pickups: Original Custom J

item.title: 【used】woofy basses / Poodle 5 Red 4.075kg【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $665
item.description:
$665 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Woofy Basses is GIB's original brand.

The reason for launching this brand was that, while running the shop, we were often asked for bass recommendations, and we needed a brand we could confidently recommend.

This model is the Poodle 5, a 5-string, 24-fret model.

It features a 34-inch scale and 17mm pitch, achieving high playability for a 5-string bass.

It's easy to handle, even for a first 5-string, and is designed to be recommended for a wide range of players.

The original pickups and passive circuit produce a straightforward sound with minimal coloration.

It's easy to use across genres and is versatile enough for everything from live performances to recording.

It also features an added finger ramp to support smooth fingering.

The vibrant color is also striking, and it's a bass that achieves a high level of playability and practicality.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some minor scratches, but overall it's in good condition.
The electronics are in good working order.
No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone
Nut width: 45.0mm
Bridge string spacing: 17.0mm
Scale: 34 inches
Year of manufacture: Unknown
Country of manufacture: CHINA

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: Soft case

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the repurchase price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

*Defect Warranty

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

*Malfunctions and damages caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

*Replacement of consumables.

*Malfunctions and damages caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

*Malfunctions and damages caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual. * If parts repaired or modified by a party other than our company malfunction or are damaged.

* If the warranty certificate does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the wording has been altered.

* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase a new instrument, you will receive a 10% discount on the sales price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the sales price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

item.title: 【new】Meridian Guitars / J-24 5 Custom 4.185kg #00511【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $5,296
item.description:
$5,296 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Meridian Guitars is a handmade bass brand based in Avezzano, Italy.

Builder Davide Cardone's basses boast a stylish, artistic design and a unique individuality.

He is committed to using the highest quality wood, seasoned over many years.

He maximizes the beauty of the wood without compromising its tonewood quality, expressing it fully on the bass.

His philosophy regarding wood extends to the hardware as well; top-of-the-line components from Delano, Bartolini, and HIPSHOT are combined according to the concept of each model.

The J-24 model features a double-cutaway body, incorporating classic characteristics.

A masterpiece combining a beautifully streamlined body, exquisite tonewood, and high-quality hardware.

The body back is made of Black Limba, and the top is made of Myrtle Burl.

Chambered construction provides a more complex sound while also resulting in a lightweight finish.

The beauty of Myartle Burl is undeniable.

It fully expresses the unique charm of wood, something that cannot be artificially replicated.

The fingerboard is Bolivian Rosewood, and the neck is Maple.

The pickups are Delano Times Square.

These pickups are truly superb Delano masterpieces. They have a wide dynamic range, very clear bass frequencies, and yet possess a warm texture.

The preamp is a John East.

The bridge is a Hipshot Style A 19mm, and the tuning machines are also Hipshot Ultralite.

The playing feel is very light, yet the sound produced is rich and full-bodied.

The low noise and refined sound image strongly suggest the high quality of the electronics, and on top of that, a straightforward and unadulterated character is strongly felt.

It's not bland at all, but captivating.

Like its appearance, it has a very elegant yet aggressive sound.

Having worked with many excellent Italian brands, Meridian is just as authentic as them. It's a top-tier bass brand.

Body Back: Black Limba
Body Top: Myrtle Burl
Fingerboard: Bolivian Rosewood
Neck: Maple
Finishing: Satin
Pickups: Delano H/H Times Square
Preamp: John East
Bridge: Hipshot A style 19mm
Tuners: Hipshot Ultralite
Controls: Vol, Bal, Hi, Hi Boost, Mid, Mid Sweep, Low, Active/passive switch, passive tone, PUs wiring switch

Delivered after being adjusted for playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Gig bag

Text: Shunsuke Saga

Inspector: Terao

-----------------------------------

Please be noted this one is USED not new.
There is not any issue on the function and the playing condition but blemishes on the cosmetic.
Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

[Slightly damaged special price]

Sadowsky's new standard series "MetroLine" is manufactured at Warwick's state-of-the-art facilities.

Roger Sadowsky's goal was to further improve his already highly successful bass design through numerous detailed refinements. With his self-developed active circuit, he succeeded in elevating the J/J bass sound to a new level. All of the characteristics of this bass—the typical Sadowsky tone, with its clear, articulate lows, modern yet not overly aggressive midrange, and clear highs—are carried over to the 21-fret vintage J/J bass, exclusively manufactured in Germany by the MetroLine series. This exceptionally lightweight bass is perfect for bassists looking for a lightweight bass that combines modern bass sound with easy handling for stage performance. It also features original parts such as hum-canceling JJ pickups, an active circuit with bypass, passive tone controls, treble and bass EQ boosts, ultra-lightweight tuners, and a responsive bridge, ensuring world-class sound and playability.

After receiving worldwide acclaim for its domestically produced models, the transition to models manufactured at the Warwick factory in Germany has become a much talked-about trend. While retaining most of the basic concepts and specifications, the new models utilize Warwick's strength, the "vast wood stock," and hand-selected materials to create lightweight bodies weighing less than 4kg, making them a departure from the original chambered NYC models. Experience this new Sadowsky challenge for yourself.

*There are some small scratches that are not visible in the image, but this does not affect the performance.

This is a custom-order model of Atelier Z's super classic model M#245, with its eye-catching vibrant blue fade color!

ATELIER Z has garnered overwhelming support from many bassists, both professional and amateur, especially those who specialize in slap playing. The flagship model, M#245, was created as a signature model for leading Japanese bassist Tomohito Aoki, and due to its high level of perfection, it remains the go-to model for many users as an all-around active JB model.

The carefully selected two-piece ash body and dense maple neck combine to produce a powerful, crisp tone. Experience the classic "V-shaped" sound, with a unique character like no other. The controls include treble and bass boost (2-band EQ), but the back panel also reveals a mid-cut trim, allowing you to fine-tune the sound to your liking.

The ATELIER Z original JBZ-4 pickups deliver a clear and powerful output, clearly capturing the attack characteristic of slap playing. The preamp is equipped with a Bartorini XTCT, delivering the powerful, compressed, and thumping Atelier Z sound in the low-mid range, truly reminiscent of Aoki himself. The use of spectrum boost wiring ensures a more powerful, impactful sound pressure without compromising the sound's character. The EQ section also features a center detent (50% boost), allowing for more precise control of the knob position.

This model boasts reliable quality and materials that can keep up with the wide range of high-end amplifiers. It's versatile enough for a wide range of styles, from slap to fingerpicking and picking, making it ideal for session players looking for a ready-to-play active guitar.

Scale: 34

Body: Ash

The M (Magunitude) #245 was released as a signature model for Tomohito Aoki, but it is now used by countless musicians, both professional and amateur, and is a standard model and a representative model of Atelier Z.

Based on the JB tone of the 70s, it boasts clear highs and a powerful low end, and the sound produced by the densely grained ash body delivers the quintessential scooped bass sound. The control system consists only of two volume controls and a 2-band EQ boost for treble and bass, but opening the back panel reveals a Mid-CUT trim, allowing you to adjust it to your preferred settings.
Attention to detail is evident in features such as the neck angle, making it easy to produce sound even with a light touch. It can handle a variety of playing styles, from the bold sound of his signature slap technique to the thick tone of fingerstyle playing and the attack of pick playing, making it a versatile instrument that can be used in any genre.
Furthermore, this model incorporates the Spectrum Boost that Mr. Aoki had been seeking, resulting in a significantly increased sense of power compared to previous models of the same type. In addition to the bold sound he is known for when slapping, it offers a new sonic potential that can handle a wide range of genres.

This is our custom-ordered model featuring a trans-white burst finish with a matching headstock and white cell binding!
Unlike regular transparent lenses, these lenses burst, allowing you to enjoy a more pronounced gradient effect.
Please try this guitar, which has a different charm from the regular M#245.

item.title: 【outlet】Jad Freer Audio / CAPO BASS PREAMP【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,080
item.description:
$1,080 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Jad Freer Audio is a brand that designs and manufactures audio equipment, founded in Italy by Mauro Freddi and Giada Pezzi.

Mauro is an expert in electronics and audio, and he handles the electronic design to meet professional specifications.

Giada supports the hardware design, and their combination results in the production of high-quality items.

Originally known for its effects pedals, the company now also manufactures amplifiers and cabinets.

While primarily supported by European musicians, it is currently experiencing a rapid increase in global attention.

The reason for this is the CAPO bass preamp.

They describe the CAPO not just as a preamp, but as a "Multiple Preamp – DI – Saturator – FX Loop."

It's a definitive pedal that becomes the core of any bassist's pedalboard.

This preamp features two different preamps, A SIDE and B SIDE, as well as two saturators with different characteristics, J and F.

A SIDE is a clean preamp with ample headroom.

It has a very natural character, strongly enhancing the tone while preserving the instrument's original sound.

The next section is the saturator.

The J and F saturators, each with different characteristics, can be freely balanced, and their mix ratio with the A SIDE preamp can also be freely adjusted.

J offers a rich low end and strong attack.

F, in contrast, is characterized by a strong midrange and a vintage-like texture.

The main concept of CAPO is the ability to freely mix the J and F saturations, which are contrasting to the extremely flat character of A SIDE.

This allows you to create a saturation effect while maintaining maximum dynamic range and shaping it to your preferred amp-like character.

This blended tone can then be further adjusted with the 3-band EQ.

BASS: ±12dB@80Hz (Shelving)
MID: ±9dB@100Hz-10KHz (Bandpass)
HIGH: ±12dB@4KHz (Shelving)

Furthermore, the DEEP switch, which boosts the low end, and the BITE switch, which enhances the attack, support even more detailed sound shaping.

Another preamp is available, the B SIDE, which uses FETs.

In contrast to the flat-response A SIDE, this has a tube amp-like tone.

Like a classic tube amp, it has a natural response, a wide dynamic range, and a wide gain range that covers from clean to overdrive.

It can be operated individually with the A SIDE, or in series using the PRE/POST switch.

In addition to the functions of this preamp, it also features DI, LPF, and loop effect functions.

The DI has two outputs.

DIRECT OUT sends a signal that bypasses the preamp, resulting in the driest sound.
PREAMP OUT sends a signal that has passed through the preamp, representing the sound after going through the preamp, EQ, and effects loop.

If an effect pedal in the effects loop inverts the phase, the phase inversion switch can be used to align the phase of DIRECT OUT and PREAMP OUT.

Using these two signals allows sound engineers greater flexibility in their work.

The LPF is a high-frequency filter designed to cut only the necessary frequencies.

It affects both PREAMP OUT and OUTPUT, even when the pedal is bypassed.

Two types of loop effects are available: PRE and POST.

PRE is placed before the pedal, and POST is placed after.

POST can be linked to A SIDE for on/off control, or kept always active.

You can see that this is not just a simple preamp, but a multi-pedal that meets many of the bassist's needs with high quality. This is the cutting edge of the world.
Please try it.

●FEATURES

• Covers everything from crystal-clear clean tones to high saturation and deep overdrive.
• A SIDE: Features a clean preamp and two high-dynamics saturators (J, F).
• Active 3-band EQ and DEEP/BITE buttons for further sound shaping.
• B SIDE: A warm-character linear FET preamp, usable in both PRE and POST modes.

• Two D.I. outputs: DIRECT OUT and PREAMP OUT
• Phase inversion switch on DIRECT OUT for readjusting the phase of balanced signals
• LPF for removing unwanted high frequencies
• FX loop PRE and FX loop POST for using pedals before and after the capo, and including their intervention in the D.I. output

●SPECS

Dimensions: 10.5 × 12.5 × 8 cm
Weight: 0.8 kg, without box
Power Supply: 9 VDC
Power Consumption: 350mA
Channels: A Side, B Side

This is an outlet item.

Due to long-term storage, there may be scratches or other cosmetic imperfections.

A warranty is provided for everything except cosmetic issues.

GIB craftsmen will rigorously inspect each item before delivery.

Accessories: Box, Instruction Manual

Text: Ryoma Terao

Product Photos: Higashie

Manufacturer's Warranty Included

item.title: Greco EB 350 1970s - Dark Cherry — $700
item.description:
$700 · Good · Low Rider

The Greco EB-350 is a vintage-style bass produced in Japan during the early 1970s (circa 1970–1974). Designed to replicate the iconic look and tone of the classic Gibson EB-3 (SG Bass) of that era, it stands as a highly popular model from the "Lawsuit Era" among vintage bass enthusiasts and Japanese instrument collectors.

​Specifications

​Body Shape: Iconic double-cutaway SG shape, crafted from Mahogany to deliver a thick, warm low-end while remaining lightweight.

​Neck & Fingerboard: Bolt-on neck made of 2 pieces Mahogany paired with a Rosewood fingerboard featuring 20 frets.

​Scale Length: A key highlight of the EB-350 is 33.5-inch long scale (similar to the Gibson EB-3L). This is longer than standard SG basses, providing better string tension and improved note clarity.

​Pickups: Loaded with vintage Maxon pickups, featuring a massive chrome-covered neck pickup ("Mudbucker") for deep, booming lows, and a dedicated bridge pickup.

​Controls: Independent 2 Volume and 2 Tone controls, with a 3-way toggle switch for pickup selection.

​Finish: Dark Cherry, beautifully revealing the natural wood grain underneath.

​Sound & Condition

​Sound: Sounds incredible. The Maxon pickups are highly powerful with strong output, making this bass easily gig-ready for any stage.

​Condition:

​The neck is in excellent condition with a fully functional truss rod.

​Plays with comfortable, low action (touching).

​The electronics and pickup system are fully functional.

​There are some dings/scratches along the body edges and bottom.

​The original pickup cover and bridge cover are missing.

​Aside from the missing covers, all other parts are original.

​Comes with its original hard shell case (matching case).

Mr. Kikuchi, who studied under Roger Sadowsky in New York and went on to popularize the Japanese-made Sadowsky brand, has launched a new brand called “Kikuchi Guitars.”

The “Hermes Series” features a solid body with a moderate weight and a compact “dinky” shape designed for modern tone and playability, pursuing the “ease of play” and “sound” that players seek.

The body is a jazz bass-style design combining an ash body with a rosewood fingerboard.

The neck grip—featuring a narrower nut width than a standard J-style—makes it easier to grip, ensuring smooth playability from the low to the high frets. Furthermore, Mr. Kikuchi’s attention to detail is evident in the design, which enhances neck strength and incorporates a headstock with just the right thickness to ensure durability.


The pickups are custom DiMarzio models, also used in the Custom Series; these hum-canceling pickups deliver a sharp, crisp sound while reducing noise.


The preamp features an original two-band equalizer; since it functions solely as a boost, it allows for intuitive sound shaping. Additionally, pulling up the tone pot switches the instrument to passive mode, further expanding your sound-shaping options.


A masterpiece, meticulously crafted one by one.

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX20 UV5 SLM/R 3.85kg #SMX A 000313-26 【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,523
item.description:
$1,523 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

This is the MX20 UV5, the first PB-type guitar in the MX series.

The body is made of lightweight solid Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro).

This is a standard combination in the MX series.

The construction has been thoroughly considered for excellent body balance and sound projection, resulting in a very refined balance that is comfortable to hold.

The back of the neck has a satin finish, providing a smooth and pleasant feel.

The pickup is a "Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup."

It has the powerful, forceful projection characteristic of PB-type basses, yet delivers a clear sound without muddy bass tones.

The preamp features the familiar 2-band EQ.

You can significantly boost the bass and treble frequencies with the touch of a button.

The circuit board of this preamp is almost identical to that of the top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen.

Copper foil shielding is also applied, resulting in surprisingly low noise.

These points are extremely significant advantages when choosing an MX series bass.

Even in this price range, Roger Sadowsky's uncompromising commitment to instrument making is evident.

While offering the ultra-smooth playability characteristic of Sadowsky, the sound is unmistakably that of a P-bass.

It outputs a modern tone that beautifully blends a robust punch with sweet, airy, and clear treble tones.

Based on the traditional PB design, this guitar incorporates modern elements, embodying Roger Sadowsky's philosophy.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Vintage 1990 Fender Japan '62 Reissue Precision Bass PBD-62 3-Tone Sunburst Made in Japan

You're looking at a used Fender Japan '62 Reissue Precision Bass PBD-62 in very good playing condition with a 3-Tone Sunburst finish.

The model name "PBD-62" refers to the Precision Bass Domestic 1962 Reissue, based on the classic 1962 Precision Bass produced for the Japanese market.

This bass was built during the famous Fujigen production era, widely regarded as the golden age of Fender Japan. Fujigen's exceptional craftsmanship and strict quality control established the worldwide reputation of Fender Japan instruments.

Today, Fujigen-era Fender Japan basses are highly sought after by musicians and collectors for their outstanding build quality, reliability, and authentic vintage tone.

item.title: 【new】De Gier / Bebop 5 Classic Ice Blue Metallic Aged 3.915kg #418【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $8,671
item.description:
$8,671 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This product has been leveled using PLEK.

Unless otherwise specified, the string height will be set at 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string) at the 12th fret.

Please feel free to let us know if you have a preferred setting.

De Gier is a Dutch brand started by Sander De Gier in 1996.

He originally focused on creating unique and modern bass guitars, but now his main products are in the Fender style.

While the brand was already highly regarded, since releasing the BeBop, a JB-style model, its exceptional quality has kept him completely occupied with that project.

Currently, in addition to the Fender style, they also offer unique models incorporating Gibson style elements.

I encountered De Gier at the Guitar Summit held in Germany in 2022.

At first glance, its appearance filled me with anticipation, and the moment I plucked the strings, that anticipation turned into certainty.

I was so overwhelmed by its incredible quality that I began negotiations.

Initially, sales to Japan were difficult for various reasons, but after persistent negotiations, we have now been able to begin sales. (It was truly amazing, and I absolutely wanted to introduce it to Japanese musicians, so I persevered for quite some time.)

Many other brands exhibited at Guitar Summit, and I spoke with various builders.

De Gier was mentioned several times, and it seemed their capabilities were widely known.

Several builders even mentioned ordering custom basses from De Gier.

That's how highly regarded the brand is in Europe and America.

And I myself share the same highest regard.

This model is their biggest hit, the Bebop.

Specifically, it's the Classic model, a passive model that aims for a more vintage style.

This product also attracted attention at the Guitar Summit held in Germany in 2025. GIB has previously stocked 4-string JB and PB models, as well as 5-string PB models, but this 5-string JB type has finally arrived.

The extensive research and development that went into creating this model is evident in its magnificent and compelling growling sound.

As a Classic model, several aspects have been changed from the standard model to more traditional specifications.

The body is made of alder, the neck is maple, and the fingerboard is rosewood—a classic combination.

The fingerboard uses a round-laminated construction.

A concern with passive 5-string basses is that they can lack openness and strong high frequencies.

This model addresses this by increasing the proportion of maple, resulting in a clearer tone.

It's clear that this model is designed with a stronger focus on the so-called 4-string characteristics and vintage style compared to the standard model.

While the standard Bebop has carbon fiber reinforcement to correct neck warping, the Classic model does not.

This gives it a traditional feel, similar to vintage Fender basses. While it has the disadvantage of not being able to achieve extremely low action, it's a clear advantage for those who prefer a different setup or are looking for a Fender-style 5-string bass.

Another distinctive feature is the thinner headstock compared to standard models.

In fact, measuring the headstock thickness of vintage Fenders reveals that it's thinner than current models and modern brands.

This model uses the same thickness as pre-CBS vintage Fenders.

It goes without saying that the sincere approach of thoroughly researching the instrument, and differentiating even the smallest specifications from standard models, contributes to the final, compelling sound.

The bridge is a classic spiral style.

The tuning machines are GOTOH RES-O-LITE, a lightweight yet relatively vintage-feeling choice.

The pickups are Lindy Fralin.

It's built with a simple passive circuit of volume and tone.

The potentiometers are CTS, and the electronics, including the wires, are finished with vintage-compliant parts.

The color is Ice Blue Metallic.

The finish is nitrocellulose lacquer, and it's finished with a relic treatment.

The excellent atmosphere created by this coloring and relic treatment is one of De Gier's signature features.

The quality is truly high.

We've included images in the product photos that convey the texture of the relic treatment.

Please take a look.

The bass strings have an open nuance, yet the treble strings don't become thin, and the sweet midrange is not lost.

Despite being a 5-string, it retains the gritty, trebly feel characteristic of a JB, and is full of fresh, vibrant intonation.

Rich in harmonics, it supports the ensemble with a strong fundamental tone—truly the ideal JB.

And the most amazing thing is that it achieves all these elements in a passive style.

This is something that can only be achieved by a builder with truly high design and technical skills.

This is exactly what we've been looking for in a passive 5-string JB.

We promise you'll be impressed.

●SPECS

Alder body
Maple neck
Rosewood board
Small frets
Bone nut
Lindy Fralin pickups
Classic Gotoh tuners
Ice Blue Metallic relic finish
Vol, Vol, Tone

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Gig bag, wrench, spec sheet, warranty card

Text: Ryoma Terao

Inspector: Kubota

Product photos: Muragishi

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan)

item.description:
$2,791.95 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A '70s Jazz Bass has arrived!!
Based on the serial number and other component specifications, wefve determined it was made in 1974.
It features an ash body and a maple neck and fingerboard?wood combinations popular on 70s Fender basses.
Although itfs a heavy instrument at 5 kg, combined with its solid neck, it delivers rich bass resonance and a crisp, clear sound.
The body has likely been refinished, and it shows dents, scratches, and weather checking consistent with its age.
The bridge fence screw holes have been filled, and there are traces of a finger rest that was added around the rear pickup.

The pickguard was custom-made by an outside workshop, and the control knobs and strap pins have been replaced with locking types (without the strap-side parts).
The tuning peg for the 3rd string is bent, and string guides have been added for the 3rd and 2nd strings.
The pickups feature a gray bobbin on the front and a black bobbin on the rear; the dates on both are illegible. The strap pin has been replaced with a Schaller lock pin (gold).

Spector USA NS-5 XL

The original Mike Inez Fishman neck pickup and matching Fishman preamp that originally came in the bass are included with the sale, allowing it to be returned to its original factory electronic configuration if desired.

Condition

The bass is in great condition and has been very well cared for. The only thing worth mentioning is a small area on the body where the satin finish has developed a slight shine from normal playing (pictured). This is just the satin finish becoming glossier from use, which is common on satin-finished instruments. It’s very hard to notice unless you have it in the exact right lighting.

Included

  • Original Spector molded hard case
  • Original Certificate of Authenticity
  • Original paperwork
  • Original tools
  • Original Mike Inez Fishman neck pickup
  • Original Mike Inez Fishman preamp

This is an outstanding USA Spector with premium roasted tonewoods, exceptional craftsmanship, and an incredibly versatile Fishman Fluence electronic setup professionally installed by Spector. item.title: 【new】De Gier / Bebop 5 Aged White 4.250kg #440【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $8,671
item.description:
$8,671 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

De Gier is a Dutch brand founded in 1996 by Sander De Gier.

He originally focused on creating unique and modern bass guitars, but now his products primarily emphasize Fender-style designs.

While the brand was already highly regarded, the release of the BeBop, a JB-style model, has been so successful that it has kept him completely occupied with production.

Currently, in addition to Fender styles, they also offer unique models incorporating Gibson styles.

I first encountered De Gier at the Guitar Summit held in Germany in 2022.

At first glance, its presence filled me with anticipation, and that feeling was confirmed the moment I strummed the strings.

I was so overwhelmed by the incredible quality that I began negotiations.

Initially, sales to Japan were difficult for various reasons, but after persistent negotiations, we have now been able to begin sales. (It was truly amazing, and I absolutely wanted to introduce it to Japanese musicians, so I persisted quite a bit.)

Many other brands exhibited at Guitar Summit, and I spoke with various builders.

De Gier came up several times, and it seemed their capabilities were widely known.

Several builders even mentioned ordering custom basses from De Gier.

That's how highly regarded the brand is in Europe and America.

And I myself hold them in the highest regard as well.

This model is their mega-hit, the BeBop 5.

Marcus Miller purchased a prototype of this model, and Richard Bona ordered one shortly after.

It continues to be highly regarded by true musicians like them.

The body is alder, and the fretboard is maple.

Its rich midrange and clear high frequencies are highly recommended.

The bridge is an original style custom-ordered from ETS, and the tuning machines are GOTOH RES-O-LITE.

The color is a vibrant and beautiful Aged White.

Their coloring skills are among the best in the world.

De Gier uses various pickups depending on the individual instrument, but this particular instrument is equipped with a Nordstrand NJ5.

This model is a pure reproduction of a pickup made by Fender in the 1960s.

While it is basically a passive circuit, it also features a custom Vanderkley Fatboost function.

By slightly boosting the low frequencies and lowering the impedance, the loss of high frequencies is reduced, resulting in a nuance that feels like both the low and high frequencies are boosted simultaneously.

The sound is already complete in passive mode, but by simply pushing it a little, it seems to gain an even more powerful impact.

This minimalist yet highly effective design is sure to impress all bassists.

The third knob is the passive tone control, but the tone effect can be adjusted using a push-pull switch.

When rolling back the tone, you might want to round off the high frequencies for a warmer sound, or conversely, strongly emphasize the high-mids.

Normally, this would be adjusted by the capacitor choice, but this function allows you to easily select either.

The fourth knob is a rotary switch, which turns the Fatboost on and off.

Despite being a 5-string bass, the 3rd and 4th strings retain a pleasant, open nuance with a nice low-mid, while the 1st and 2nd strings retain a harmonically rich midrange.

And the 5th string retains the trebly, characteristic JB feel, overflowing with a fresh, vibrant tone.

This is exactly what we've been looking for in a 5-string JB.

We promise you'll be impressed.

●SPECS

Alder Body
Maple Neck
Maple Fingerboard
Dots Inlays
Bone Nut
Nordstrand NJ5 Pickups
Aged White

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

item.title: Peavey TL-Five 1989 - Red — $1,200
item.description:
$1,200 · Good · ATXBass

This TL was owned by Stu Cook (of CCR fame) when he was playing for the band Southern Pacific. It has plenty of small dings etc., but for as well travelled as this bass is, it really is in good shape. After I got it, I had to have the frets leveled, crowned and polished. It currently plays like a dream. I replaced the stock preamp with a Tone Monster, but I believe I still have the stock one that I will include in the sale. I also have the case. It’s pretty beat up but usable. I also have the documents of authenticity. Bass weighs in right around 9 1/2lbs. I have included a video of Stu playing the bass in Hawaii All those years ago.

item.title: Warwick Rockbass Artist Line Bootsy Collins Space Bass 4 String Electric Bass Guitar - Bootsy Purple — $1,375
item.description:
$1,375 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

The Warwick RockBass Artist Line Bootsy Collins "Space Bass" is an exceptional 4-string electric bass that combines the legendary funk sound and the unmistakable style of Bootsy Collins in one spectacular instrument. With its iconic star design, high-quality features and striking Purple Bootsy High Polish finish, this bass is not only a sonic powerhouse, but also an absolute eye-catcher on any stage. Designed for bassists looking for performance, character and extravagant design, the Space Bass combines classic Warwick quality with unique Artist Line style.
The two-piece mahogany body ensures a warm, powerful basic sound with powerful mids and long sustain. The 3-strip laminated maple neck with black veneer strips, together with the integrated carbon reinforcements, offers high stability and a fast, comfortable playing feel. The high-quality wenge fingerboard with eye-catching star inlays underlines the individual look of the instrument and at the same time delivers a direct response and precise articulation. Thanks to the bolt-on construction and the ergonomic long-scale scale, the bass impresses with excellent playability and balanced handling.
In terms of sound, the Warwick RockBass Bootsy Collins Space Bass delivers maximum versatility and powerful presence. The active MEC Jazz / Twin Jazz pickups produce a clear, defined tone with a rich foundation and brilliant assertiveness. Using the active MEC 2-band electronics with volume, balance, treble and bass controls, the sound can be flexibly adapted to a wide variety of music styles - from funk and soul to rock and modern bass sounds. High-quality shielding and precise electronics ensure a clean and interference-free signal.
Warwick also relies on the highest quality when it comes to hardware and workmanship. The 2-part Warwick 3D bridge allows for precise adjustment options and optimal intonation, while Warwick tuners and Warwick S-Security Locks ensure maximum reliability on stage. Rounded fret ends with Invisible Fretwork Technology further increase playing comfort. The scope of delivery also includes a wide range of accessories and a high-quality RockBass Starline gig bag.

Features:

Warwick RockBass Artist Line Bootsy Collins "Space Bass"

4-string electric bass

34" long scale

2-piece solid Mahogany body

3-piece laminated Maple neck with black veneer stripes

Bootsy Collins "Star Shaped" Pickguard

Two carbon bars for neck reinforcement, from 12th to 24th fret

2-way trussrod

Bolt-on construction

Wenge fingerboard with Star inlays

20" radius

Nut width 38.5 mm / 1.5"

24 jumbo titanium-brass alloy frets (width: 2.9 mm / height: 1.4 mm)

Ball end frets with IFT (Invisible Fret Technology)

Warwick Just-A-Nut III Tedur

Active MEC Jazz / Twin Jazz pickups

Active MEC 2-band electronics

Controls for Volume (P/P) / Balance / Treble and Bass (stacked)

Copper shielded electronic compartment, pickup routings with shielding paint

Threaded inserts for pickup screws

Warwick machine heads

2-piece Warwick 3D bridge

String spacing 19 mm / 3/4"

Distance E to G string 57 mm / 2 1/4"

Warwick S-Security Locks

Warwick RED Strings (42200 M) .045"-.105"

Purple Bootsy High Polish finish

Chrome hardware

Case: Gig bag included

Weight: 9 lb 6 oz

item.title: Warwick RockBass Corvette Multiscale 5-string Bass Guitar - Black — $999
item.description:
$999 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

Get your thumping, heart-pumping bass career started on another note with the Warwick RockBass Corvette Multi-scale Bass Guitar. With Warwick’s years of experience crafting high-quality, tour-tested, growling basses, the Corvette Multi-scale brings honor to the RockBass family line with these same features in droves. Apart from its stunning design, this alder-body monster boasts some killer tone thanks to its MEC Active J-style bass pickups perched at the neck and bridge. The RockBass Corvette Multi-scale offers some excellent tone-shaping capabilities thanks to balance, bass, and treble knobs. Also, you'll love traveling the sleek and smooth multi-scale wenge fretboard that makes traveling up and down across all five strings a walk in the park. The reviews are in here at Sweetwater: the RockBass Corvette Multi-scale absolutely rocks!

Maple neck and multi-scale wenge fingerboard offer a smooth surface for fretting and fast harmonic runs

"C"-shaped neck and 26-inch radius fingerboard feels comfortable in the hands

Power and growl from MEC Active J-style pickups

2-piece Warwick bridge delivers vibration of strings to the body and is easily adjusted

Back panel quick access electronics compartment makes accessing electronics or changing battery quick and simple

Comfortable and beloved body shape gigs like a dream with tone to match

item.title: Warwick RockBass Streamer NT I 4-string Bass Guitar - Black — $999
item.description:
$999 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

Warwick’s RockBass Streamer NT I is a high-performance 4-string that represents a wallet-friendly entryway into the tone-enhancing world of neck-through bass design! A core of maple with ekanga veneer stripes forms the neck-through portion of this bass, with a pair of lightweight alder wings and wenge fingerboard for a warm, balanced tone. This impressive construction is taken to the next level with an active MEC P/J pickup configuration that bassists find covers just about every low-end application out there — from Warwick’s signature growl to warm, rounded-off fingerstyle tones. Finally, it wouldn’t be a Warwick without rock-solid hardware, and the Tedur Just-A-Nut III, the 3D 2-piece bridge, and the high-quality Warwick machine heads ensure that your Streamer NT I’s setup is both easy to adjust and exceptionally stable!


Features:

Maple and ekanga neck-through design delivers excellent sustain and blooming resonance

Alder wings provide a balanced tone and lightweight feel

Wenge fingerboard studded with 24 jumbo nickel-silver frets in a 20-inch radius for effortless playability

Comfortable curved body shape

Active MEC P/J pickups cover a massive range of tonal applications

Rock-solid Warwick 3D 2-piece bridge for enhanced string-to-body contact

Tedur Just-A-Nut III allows for nut-action adjustment without files or permanent modification

Active electronics allow for deep tone-sculpting possibilities

item.title: Fender Made in Japan Limited International Color Precision Bass Guitar Morocco Red 2023 - Morocco Red — $899
item.description:
$899 · Excellent · poison_bass

Up for sale is a 2023 Fender Made in Japan Limited International Color Series Precision Bass in Morocco Red — a vibrant, limited-run reissue of Fender's rare early-1980s International Color series, built at Fender's Japan factory with vintage-inspired specs and a striking, hard-to-find color.

This bass is in excellent — no dings, dents, or scratches, barely played. The neck is a joy to play, with a slender profile that splits the difference between a Jazz Bass and Precision Bass feel. Manufacturing quality control on this bass punches well above its price point — Fender Mexico-level pricing with build quality that rivals or beats USA Fender. Sound is full and clear with classic P-Bass roundness and plenty of attack.

Weighs 7.8 pounds (ultra light)

Includes: original gig bag

Fender Made in Japan Limited International Color Precision Bass Features:

  • Limited colors named after world cities
  • Based on the Fender Made in Japan Traditional Series, first released in the '80s
  • Basswood body delivers classic P-Bass tone and sustain
  • Vintage appointments and '70s-style specs: Modern Logo, 4-saddle vintage-style bridge, thumb rest, vintage-style tuners
  • 34" scale length, "U"-shaped neck, and 9.5" fingerboard radius make for a supremely playable, comfortable maple neck
  • Vintage-style split single-coil pickup for classic P-Bass tone with plenty of musicality and realism

item.description:
$2,439.89 · Brand New · KUROSAWA MUSIC G-CLUB UMEDA

Welcome to Kurosawa Music G-CLUB UMEDA!!

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――――


Xotic was founded in California, USA, in 1996. The company’s models are highly regarded in professional settings.

While drawing inspiration from vintage styles, Xotic pursues the playing feel and sound that modern players truly desire, delivering an instantly comfortable grip, stress-free playability, and an incredibly user-friendly sound.

Building on a passive-like, natural tone, the natural 3-band EQ and active circuitry allow the guitar to seamlessly handle everything from warm vintage sounds to modern hi-fi tones.


The model we’ve just received is the XJ-1T 5-string, which we specially ordered. The body color, “Sea Foam Green,” features an exquisite shade that’s a vivid green with just a hint of blue. It’s not too flashy, yet it has a strong presence that really catches the eye on stage—a look you’ll love for years to come.


Sound-wise, the lows are thick without being overly bloated, retaining clear definition; the mids have just the right amount of punch; and the highs cut through naturally, just as much as needed.

As a result, it won’t get lost in a band ensemble. It’s easy to handle regardless of whether you’re recording or performing live, and it serves as a versatile main bass for any genre.


The EQ is also excellent; with just a slight adjustment, it effortlessly dials in the exact sound you’re looking for. The tone stays cohesive without needing major corrections, and it responds naturally to the fine-tuning required in any situation—whether you want “a bit more body,” “better projection for slapping,” or “a bit more presence.” This means you’ll rarely struggle with tone shaping even when switching amps or venues, which is one of Xotic’s greatest appeals.


It’s not a bass with a flashy, in-your-face character, but that’s precisely what gives it the versatility to handle any gig.

That’s why it’s trusted across a wide range of scenarios—from live performances to recording and sessions—regardless of genre, and continues to be chosen as the main bass by many bassists.


In short, it’s the epitome of versatility.

Please take this opportunity to experience Xotic’s uncompromising quality—in looks, playability, and sound—for yourself.


[SPEC]

Body: Alder

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Pickups: Raw Vintage w/Wood Cover

Preamp: Xotic Onboard Bass Preamp

Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone

　　　　　3-Band EQ Xotic Onboard Bass Preamp with 2 FQ selector switches

　　　　　Neck Volume: Push (Active) / Pull (Passive)

　　　　　Bridge Volume: Push (Parallel) / Pull (Series)

　　　　　Switch 1 (Mid Frequency Selector): UP/DOWN - 400Hz / 800Hz - ±12dB

　　　　　Switch 2 (Treble Frequency Selector): UP/DOWN - 6KHz / 12K - ±18dB

Weight: 4.19 kg

item.title: Kikuchi Guitars Hermes M70-4 Natural 【4.36kg】 — $2,837.08
item.description:
$2,837.08 · Brand New · KUROSAWA MUSIC G-CLUB UMEDA

Welcome to Kurosawa Music G-CLUB UMEDA!!

We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Please contact us for more information.


*Important Notice!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we cannot accept PayPal Guest Checkout.

Please create a PayPal account before making payment.

Also, please be sure to read our store policies listed below the instrument description!

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――――



Mr. Kikuchi, who studied under Roger Sadowsky in New York and went on to popularize the Japanese-made Sadowsky brand, has launched a new brand: “Kikuchi Guitars.”


The new “70” series has been introduced, featuring a full-size body, a 20-fret neck, and ’70s-style pickup placement—each element blending “exquisitely” to produce a distinctive tone.


Unlike the versatile standard Hermes series, this series incorporates traditional ’70s specifications throughout, delivering rich low-end and crisp, punchy high-end. The combination of two volume controls allows players to freely adjust the volume of each pickup, greatly expanding the range of tonal variations they can create.


The 20-fret fingerboard produces a crisp, snappy, and punchy tone during slap playing.


The slightly curved 10″ R fingerboard makes it easy to produce comfortable ghost notes, allowing players to achieve the powerful “chop tone” that many envision.


To accommodate a wide range of playing styles and musical genres, the string action is set uniformly across all fret positions, delivering a powerful tone for aggressive picking and a warm, enveloping tone for gentle, sweeping fingering.


The full-size body shape absorbs sharp, forceful input to produce a powerful tone. It features the deep, rich bass characteristic of a full-size body, creating a thick, warm, and natural wooden resonance.


The body is crafted from ash. Its dry, punchy tone blends exquisitely with the rich, powerful sound of the full-size body.


The bridge pickup features a ’70s-style placement, positioned closer to the bridge. When mixed with the neck pickup, it produces a clear, crisp tone.


Additionally, when fingerpicking with your fingers resting on the bridge pickup, your picking position naturally shifts toward the bridge, allowing you to produce a tight, well-separated tone.


Body: Full Size / 2-Piece Ash (Solid)

Neck: Maple, Thin Matte Nitro

Fingerboard: Maple

Fingerboard Radius: 10″ (254R)

Nut Material: Tusq

Nut Width: 37mm

Frets: DHP24H, 20F

Scale: 864mm (34″)

Body Finish: Poly

Hardware Color: Chrome

Bridge: Hipshot 20mm Spacing

Tuners: Hipshot License Ultra-Light

Pickups: Original Custom J (’70s locations)

Controls: Neck Volume

Bridge Volume

Passive Roll-Off Tone (with pull switch for preamp bypass)

Treble/Bass Boost


Comes with a semi-hard case
